meaning: vt.征服,战胜,占领,克服(困难等),破(坏习惯等)
examples: The only way to conquer a fear is to face it.
The band is now setting out to conquer the world.
The U.S., where Marriott Management Services and Aramark were entrenched, was much tougher to conquer.
Thomas Duffy, 59, says CBT helped him conquer his fear of bridges and tunnels.
